message.content:
You are a world-class software architect (Netflix + AWS + Google + Apple level, 200 years of experience).

Task: Create an implementation plan for RECO-813.

Working directory: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813
Plan folder: .claude/plans/RECO-813/ (in main repo: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.claude/plans/RECO-813/)

CRITICAL: All file operations for CODE must happen in the worktree (/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/). Plan files live in the main repo's .claude/plans/RECO-813/.

Read these files before starting:
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/CLAUDE.md
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.claude/plans/RECO-813/requirements.md

## Task Summary

RECO-813: "return logs back for 3rd party requests"

The goal is to add/restore `@LogExecution` decorator to 3rd party method calls in:
1. `src/modules/rest/trending-products/` — all services/files
2. `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/` — all services/files

The `@LogExecution` decorator is already defined in `src/decorators/log-execution.decorator.ts`. We need to identify ALL methods that make 3rd party calls (external APIs, Algolia, Redis cache operations, etc.) and wrap them with `@LogExecution`.

## What to investigate

Please explore the following files thoroughly and identify which methods are 3rd party calls that need `@LogExecution`:

**Trending Products module:**
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/trending-products/trending-products.service.ts
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/trending-products/render-trending-results.service.ts
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/trending-products/trending-products-dashboard.controller.ts

**Frequently Bought Together module:**
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts

Also explore for context/patterns:
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/decorators/log-execution.decorator.ts
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/related-products/ (for reference patterns where @LogExecution is already used)
- /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/addons/ (for reference patterns)

## Plan Output

Create the implementation plan at:
/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.claude/plans/RECO-813/implementation-plan.md

The plan must include:
1. **Summary** — what and why
2. **Scope** — single PR, files to modify
3. **Ordered task breakdown** — specific, atomic tasks with exact file paths, method names, and what decorator options to use. Each task must be 2-5 minutes of work.
4. **Test strategy** — which unit test files to update/create
5. **Risk assessment** — what could break

For each method that needs `@LogExecution`, specify:
- The exact method name
- Which file it's in
- What `@LogExecution` options to use (logParams: true/false, logResponse: true/false, responseFormatter if response is large, etc.)
- Why it's a 3rd party call

Do NOT write any code. Just produce the plan.

After creating the plan, also update the progress tracker at:
/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.claude/plans/RECO-813/progress-tracker.md
— mark Step 7 as [x] with timestamp and a brief summary of the plan.

content.text: I'll start by reading the required files and exploring the codebase to understand the patterns and identify all methods that need `@LogExecution`.

1	# Requirements: RECO-813
2	
3	## Ticket
4	**Summary:** return logs back for 3rd party requests
5	**JIRA:** https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-813
6	**Status:** To Do → In Progress
7	**Sprint:** RECO 6 (ends 2026-04-08)
8	**Parent:** RECO-36 (Tech debt, refactoring, improvements)
9	**Labels:** FOR_CC
10	
11	## Description (from user context — ticket body is placeholder)
12	
13	Restore/add logging for 3rd party requests in the trending-products and frequently-bought-together modules. Logging was likely removed or never added. Goal: ensure 3rd party calls are visible in production logs.
14	
15	## Scope (user-specified)
16	- `src/modules/rest/trending-products/` — find all 3rd party requests, wrap with `@LogExecution`
17	- `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/` — find all 3rd party requests, wrap with `@LogExecution`
18	- `docs/modules/related-products/` — reference for understanding patterns (read-only)
19	- Use `@LogExecution` decorator to wrap 3rd party method calls
20	- Set log level to `info` where needed
21	
22	## Acceptance Criteria
23	- All 3rd party service calls in trending-products and frequently-bought-together modules are wrapped with `@LogExecution`
24	- Logs appear at `info` level (or appropriate level) in production
25	- No existing functionality broken
26	
27	## Notes
28	- The `@LogExecution` decorator is already defined in `src/decorators/log-execution.decorator.ts`
29	- No new env vars required
30	- No DB changes required
31	- This is a pure logging improvement — no business logic changes
32

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Tools & Requirements
6	
7	- **Node.js**: >=v24.13.0 (see `.node-version`, `.nvmrc`)
8	- **npm**: >=11.0.0
9	- **Core stack**: NestJS, Fastify, MySQL, Knex.js, TypeScript (strict mode)
10	- **Testing**: Jest (unit/e2e), Artillery (load testing)
11	- **Code quality**: ESLint v9+ (flat config), Prettier, Husky
12	- **Monitoring**: New Relic, Rollbar
13	- **CLI**: nest-commander
14	- **Cloud**: AWS SDK (SQS), PostgreSQL (Redshift), Redis (ioredis), Redlock
15	- **AI/ML**: OpenAI API integration
16	- **Search**: Algolia
17	
18	## Key Commands
19	
20	```bash
21	# Development
22	npm run start:dev          # Hot reload
23	npm run 🐎                 # Hot reload (horse emoji shortcut)
24	npm run build              # Production build
25	npm run start:prod         # Production start
26	
27	# Testing
28	npm run test               # Full suite (lint, format, type-check, unit, e2e)
29	npm run test:unit          # Unit tests only
30	npm run test:e2e          # E2E tests (auto DB reset)
31	npm run test:coverage      # Full suite with coverage reports
32	npm run test:unit:coverage # Unit tests with coverage
33	npm run test:e2e:coverage  # E2E tests with coverage
34	
35	# Run a single test file
36	dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --config ./test/jest-unit.config.ts path/to/file.unit-spec.ts
37	dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --runInBand --forceExit --config ./test/jest-e2e.config.ts path/to/file.e2e-spec.ts
38	
39	# Database
40	npm run migrate            # Run pending migrations
41	npm run migrate:prod       # Production migrations
42	npm run migrate:make name  # Create migration
43	npm run migrate:up         # Migrate one up
44	npm run migrate:down       # Migrate one down
45	npm run migrate:rollback   # Rollback last batch
46	npm run db:reset:test     # Reset test DB
47	
48	# Code Quality
49	npm run code-quality-check # Run lint, format, and type-check
50	npm run lint              # Check linting
51	npm run lint:fix          # Auto-fix linting
52	npm run format            # Format code
53	npm run type-check        # TypeScript check
54	
55	# CLI & Load Testing
56	npm run cli:dev           # Run CLI commands (dev)
57	npm run cli:prod          # Run CLI commands (prod)
58	npm run artillery-load-test  # Load testing
59	```
60	
61	## Architecture
62	
63	**NestJS microservice** with MySQL replication, strict TypeScript, modular domain organization, AWS SQS integration, Redis caching, and AI-powered recommendations.
64	
65	### Project Structure
66	
67	```
68	src/
69	├── modules/
70	│   ├── rest/              # API endpoints
71	│   │   ├── addons/       # Product addon recommendations
72	│   │   ├── email-recommendations/  # Email-based recommendations
73	│   │   ├── frequently-bought-together/  # FBT recommendations
74	│   │   ├── load-test/    # Load testing endpoints
75	│   │   ├── open-ai/      # OpenAI integration
76	│   │   ├── process-style/ # Style processing
77	│   │   ├── promos/       # Promotional content
78	│   │   ├── promotional-products/  # Promo product recommendations
79	│   │   ├── related-products/  # Related product recommendations
80	│   │   ├── robots/       # Robots.txt
81	│   │   └── trending-products/  # Trending product recommendations
82	│   ├── db/               # Database models & services
83	│   │   ├── category-correlations/
84	│   │   ├── condensed-features/
85	│   │   ├── forecasted-trending-products/
86	│   │   ├── product-annual-sales/
87	│   │   ├── promotional-products/
88	│   │   ├── redshift/     # Redshift data integration
89	│   │   │   ├── orders/
90	│   │   │   └── trending-items/
91	│   │   ├── root-categories-correlations/
92	│   │   ├── default-unit-prices/
93	│   │   ├── products/
94	│   │   ├── style-alternatives/
95	│   │   ├── style-colors/
96	│   │   ├── styles/
97	│   │   ├── styles-colors/
98	│   │   ├── styles-hash/
99	│   │   └── styles-mv/
100	│   ├── cli/              # CLI commands
101	│   │   ├── commands/     # Command implementations
102	│   │   └── workers/      # Background workers
103	│   ├── app/              # Application module
104	│   ├── algolia/          # Algolia search integration
105	│   ├── cache/            # Redis caching
106	│   ├── health/           # Health checks
107	│   ├── metrics/          # Metrics module
108	│   ├── mms/              # MMS integration
109	│   ├── mms-styles-sync/  # MMS style synchronization
110	│   ├── newrelic/         # New Relic monitoring
111	│   ├── product-hydration/ # Product hydration
112	│   └── env-config/       # Environment configuration
113	├── dto/                  # Data Transfer Objects by feature
114	│   ├── addons/
115	│   ├── brand-products/
116	│   ├── email-recommendations/
117	│   ├── frequently-bought-together/
118	│   ├── load-test/
119	│   ├── nlp-features/
120	│   ├── related-products/
121	│   └── trending-products/
122	├── utils/                # Utility functions
123	├── middlewares/          # Custom middlewares
124	├── interfaces/           # TypeScript interfaces
125	├── decorators/           # Custom decorators
126	├── interceptors/         # Custom interceptors
127	├── guards/               # Auth guards
128	├── constants/            # Constants and enums
129	├── configs/              # Configuration files
130	├── scripts/              # Automation & evaluation scripts
131	│   ├── evaluator-related-products/
132	│   ├── evaluator-fbt/
133	│   └── load-testing/
134	├── migrations/           # Database migrations
135	├── seeds/                # Database seeds
136	└── unit-tests/          # Unit tests
137	test/                    # E2E tests
138	```
139	
140	### Import Aliases (tsconfig.json)
141	
142	```typescript
143	@config/*        → src/configs/*
144	@constants/*     → src/constants/*
145	@modules/*       → src/modules/*
146	@utils/*         → src/utils/*
147	@middlewares/*   → src/middlewares/*
148	@services/*      → src/services/*
149	@interfaces/*    → src/interfaces/*
150	@decorators/*    → src/decorators/*
151	@dto/*           → src/dto/*
152	@guards/*        → src/guards/*
153	@interceptors/*  → src/interceptors/*
154	@frontend-tests/* → src/frontend-tests/*
155	@seeds/*         → src/seeds/*
156	```
157	
158	### Database Models
159	
160	The service includes comprehensive database models with full CRUD operations:
161	
162	- **styles**: Core product styling data with UUID primary key
163	- **style_colors**: Color information (style_id, color_id, color_rgb, color_name)
164	- **styles_hash**: Hash-based style identification (style_id, hash, hashing_algorithm)
165	- **condensed_features**: Compressed feature data (style_id, condensed_features JSON)
166	- **category_correlations**: Category relationship data (primary_category_id, edge_id, edge_weight)
167	- **root_categories_correlations**: Root-level category relationships
168	- **forecasted_trending_products**: ML-forecasted trending products
169	- **product_annual_sales**: Annual sales data for products
170	- **promotional_products**: Promotional product data
171	- **redshift_orders**: Order data from Redshift
172	- **redshift_trending_items**: Trending items data from Redshift
173	
174	Each model includes:
175	
176	- Base, Create, and Update DTOs in `/dto/` folders or module folders
177	- TypeScript interfaces in `/interfaces/` folders
178	- Service layer with read/write database separation
179	- Soft deletes with `deleted_at` timestamp (where applicable)
180	- Proper indexing and unique constraints
181	
182	### CLI Commands
183	
184	Available CLI commands (via `npm run cli:dev`):
185	
186	- **Migration Commands**: `migrate`, `migrate-up`, `migrate-down`, `migrate-rollback`
187	- **Data Population**: `populate-category-correlations`, `populate-trending-items`
188	- **Forecasting**: `forecast-trending-products`
189	- **Synchronization**: `sync-source-styles`
190	- **Data Preparation**: `prepare-promo-seed`
191	
192	## Documentation
193	
194	### Where to Find Business Logic Docs
195	
196	All documentation lives in `docs/`. When working on a feature or investigating how something works, check here first:
197	
198	| Document                                            | What it covers                                           |
199	| --------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
200	| `docs/development.md`                               | Local dev setup, bin commands                            |
201	| `docs/database-migrations.md`                       | Migration commands, file structure, production flow      |
202	| `docs/devcontainer-mysql-replica.md`                | Local MySQL write/read replication setup                 |
203	| `docs/cron-jobs.md`                                 | Scheduled jobs, how to add new ones                      |
204	| `docs/retry-util.md`                                | `withRetry` utility — options, examples                  |
205	| `docs/algolia-style-sync-to-nlp.md`                 | NLP feature generation from Algolia via OpenAI (V1 & V2) |
206	| `docs/push-condensed-styles-features-to-algolia.md` | Pushing NLP features back to Algolia index               |
207	| `docs/sync-mms-alternatives-to-algolia.md`          | Syncing MMS style alternatives to Algolia                |
208	| `docs/reco-models-evaluation-scripts.md`            | Evaluation pipelines for Related Products and FBT models |
209	| `docs/klaviyo-email-recommendations-setup.md`       | Email recommendations integration with Klaviyo           |
210	| `docs/db-schema/`                                   | Database schema reference                                |
211	| `docs/modules/related-products/V1/overview.md`      | Related Products model — data sync, algorithm, endpoints |
212	| `docs/modules/fbt/V1/overview.md`                   | FBT V1 — category correlation model                      |
213	| `docs/modules/fbt/V2/overview.md`                   | FBT V2 — co-purchase data model                          |
214	| `docs/modules/trending/V1/overview.md`              | Trending Products — forecasting pipeline and serving     |
215	| `rfc/`                                              | RFCs — architecture decisions and feature proposals      |
216	
217	### Documentation Policy
218	
219	**Every PR that introduces new functionality or modifies existing logic must include a corresponding update or creation of documentation in `docs/`.**
220	
221	- New feature → create a new doc or add a section to an existing one
222	- Changed logic → update the relevant existing doc
223	- New recommendation model version → add under `docs/modules/{model}/V{n}/`
224	- No feature is considered complete without documentation
225	
226	## Development Rules
227	
228	### Code Style
229	
230	- **NO COMMENTS**, **NO EXPLANATIONS** in code (except ESLint disable commands)
231	- **ALWAYS INSPECT EXISTING FILES** before creating new ones
232	- Match exact patterns from existing files:
233	  - `.model.ts` → check `/src/modules/db/*/models/`
234	  - `.service.ts` → check similar modules
235	  - `.controller.ts` → check `/src/modules/rest/`
236	  - `.dto.ts` → check `/src/dto/` or module folders
237	  - `.util.ts` → check `/src/utils/`
238	  - `.unit-spec.ts` → check `/src/unit-tests/`
239	  - `.e2e-spec.ts` → check `/test/`
240	  - `.interface.ts` → check `/src/interfaces/`
241	
242	### Environment Variables (CRITICAL)
243	
244	**ALWAYS follow this complete process when adding new environment variables:**
245	
246	#### Step 1: Add to Environment Files
247	
248	Add the new variable to env files with documentation:
249	
250	```bash
251	# .env
252	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
253	```
254	
255	```bash
256	# .env.test
257	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
258	```
259	
260	```bash
261	# .env.example
262	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
263	```
264	
265	```bash
266	# .env.test.example
267	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=test_value # Use appropriate test value
268	```
269	
270	#### Step 2: Create Constant Export
271	
272	Add constant to `src/constants/env-variables.constant.ts`:
273	
274	```typescript
275	export const NEW_VARIABLE_NAME = 'NEW_VARIABLE_NAME';
276	```
277	
278	#### Step 3: Add Validation Rule
279	
280	Add validation decorator to `src/configs/env-validations.config.ts` in the `EnvironmentVariables` class:
281	
282	```typescript
283	@IsString() // Use appropriate validator: @IsString(), @IsNumber(), @IsBoolean(), @IsEnum()
284	@IsNotEmpty() // Add constraints: @Min(), @Max(), etc.
285	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: string; // Use appropriate type
286	```
287	
288	**Common validators:**
289	
290	- `@IsString()` - String values
291	- `@IsNumber()` - Numeric values
292	- `@IsBoolean()` - Boolean values
293	- `@IsEnum(EnumType)` - Enum values
294	- `@IsNotEmpty()` - Required field
295	- `@Min(value)` / `@Max(value)` - Numeric constraints
296	- `@IsUrl()` - URL validation
297	
298	#### Step 4: Add to Environment Loader
299	
300	Add to `src/utils/get-env-file.util.ts` in the `getEnvVariables()` function:
301	
302	```typescript
303	export const getEnvVariables = (): Record<string, string | number | undefined> => ({
304		// ... existing variables
305		NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: process.env['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
306	});
307	```
308	
309	#### Step 5: Add Test Coverage
310	
311	Add test cases to `test/env-config.module.e2e-spec.ts`:
312	
313	```typescript
314	// In beforeAll hook
315	testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'] = String(envConfig['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME']);
316	
317	// In test case
318	it('should load NEW_VARIABLE_NAME correctly', () => {
319		expect(envConfigService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME })).toBe(
320			testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
321		);
322	});
323	```
324	
325	#### Usage: Two Approaches
326	
327	**Approach 1: EnvConfigService (Dependency Injection)**
328	
329	```typescript
330	import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
331	import { EnvConfigService } from '@modules/env-config/env-config.service';
332	import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';
333	
334	@Injectable()
335	export class MyService {
336		constructor(private readonly configService: EnvConfigService) {}
337	
338		someMethod(): void {
339			const value = this.configService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
340			const numericValue = this.configService.get<number>({
341				key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME,
342				converter: Number,
343			});
344		}
345	}
346	```
347	
348	**Approach 2: getEnv Utility (Standalone Usage)**
349	
350	```typescript
351	import { getEnv } from '@utils/get-env-variable.util';
352	import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';
353	
354	const value = getEnv<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
355	const numericValue = getEnv<number>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME, converter: Number });
356	```
357	
358	**Important Notes:**
359	
360	- **ALWAYS** complete ALL 5 steps - missing any step will cause validation failures
361	- **NEVER** access `process.env` directly in application code
362	- **ALWAYS** use constants from `@constants/env-variables.constant`
363	- **TEST** your changes by running `npm run test:e2e`
364	- Validation runs automatically on app startup and will fail fast if misconfigured
365	- For production, environment variables are loaded from system environment
366	- For dev/test, variables are loaded from `.env` and `.env.test` files
367	
368	### Database Migrations (CRITICAL — Read Before Writing Any Migration)
369	
370	Full guide: **[docs/database/zero-downtime-migrations.md](./docs/database/zero-downtime-migrations.md)**
371	
372	**ktool deployment order**: new code pods start → health checks pass → migration runs → old pods drain.
373	This means new code runs against the old schema until the migration completes.
374	
375	#### Breaking Changes Require 3 Releases
376	
377	Any of the following **MUST** follow the **3-phase release process** (each phase is a separate release):
378	
379	| Forbidden in single release | Use instead |
380	|-----------------------------|-------------|
381	| `table.dropColumn(...)` | Phase 1: stop writing → Phase 3: drop |
382	| `table.renameColumn(...)` | Phase 1: add new col → Phase 2: dual-write → Phase 3: drop old |
383	| Column type change (incompatible) | Phase 1: add new col → Phase 2: dual-write → Phase 3: drop old |
384	| `notNullable()` on existing column without default | Phase 1: nullable → Phase 2: backfill → Phase 3: add constraint |
385	| `dropTable(...)` | Phase 1: remove all code refs → Phase 3: drop |
386	
387	#### Safe in a Single Release
388	
389	- Adding a nullable column
390	- Adding a column with a default value
391	- Creating a new table
392	- Adding an index (use `ALGORITHM=INPLACE, LOCK=NONE` for large tables)
393	- Widening a column (e.g. VARCHAR 100→255)
394	
395	#### Phase 2 Code Rules (dual-write + null guard)
396	
397	When deploying Phase 2, new code **must** handle the old schema (migration hasn't run yet):
398	
399	```typescript
400	// Writing — dual-write keeps old pods safe during drain
401	await knex(TABLE_NAME).update({
402	  old_col: value,   // old pods still read this
403	  new_col: value,   // new column
404	});
405	
406	// Reading — new_col may be null before migration backfills it
407	const value = record.new_col ?? record.old_col;
408	```
409	
410	#### PR Title Convention for 3-Phase Changes
411	
412	```
413	RECO-1234 [Phase 1]: Add new_col to table_name
414	RECO-1234 [Phase 2]: Dual-write new_col, backfill rows
415	RECO-1234 [Phase 3]: Drop old_col from table_name
416	```
417	
418	#### Writers That Must Be Updated
419	
420	HTTP controllers in `src/modules/rest/` are **read-only**. All DB writers:
421	- Cron jobs: `src/modules/cli/commands/`
422	- Event handlers: `src/modules/*/` services
423	- Stored procedures: `src/migrations/*_procedure.ts`
424	
425	### Git Workflow
426	
427	- **Branches**: `RECO-XXXX-suffix`
428	- **Commits**: `RECO-XXXX: Description in imperative mood`
429	- **PRs**: `RECO-XXXX: Short description`, requires Code Owner approval, squash merge only
430	
431	### Named Parameters (CRITICAL)
432	
433	**ALL functions must use named (object destructured) parameters — no positional parameters.**
434	
435	```typescript
436	// Good
437	async findByStyle({ styleId, colorId }: { styleId: string; colorId: number }): Promise<Result> {}
438	
439	// Bad
440	async findByStyle(styleId: string, colorId: number): Promise<Result> {}
441	```
442	
443	### Database Read/Write Pattern
444	
445	Always inject both connections; use `knexRead` for SELECT and `knexWrite` for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE:
446	
447	```typescript
448	@Injectable()
449	export class YourModel {
450	  constructor(
451	    @InjectConnection(KNEX_READ_CONNECTION) private readonly knexRead: Knex,
452	    @InjectConnection(KNEX_WRITE_CONNECTION) private readonly knexWrite: Knex,
453	  ) {}
454	}
455	```
456	
457	### Common Patterns
458	
459	```typescript
460	// Logging
461	import { Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
462	export class ClassName {
463	  private readonly logger = new Logger(ClassName.name);
464	}
465	
466	// Swagger
467	@ApiOperation({ summary: 'Description' })
468	@ApiResponse({ status: HttpStatus.OK, description: 'Success response' })
469	```
470	
471	### LogExecution Decorator (CRITICAL)
472	
473	**ALWAYS wrap important methods and 3rd party requests with `@LogExecution` decorator.**
474	
475	The `@LogExecution` decorator provides comprehensive logging and monitoring for method execution:
476	
477	- **Performance tracking**: Logs execution time with warnings if exceeds threshold
478	- **Parameter logging**: Logs method input parameters
479	- **Response logging**: Logs method output
480	- **Error tracking**: Logs errors and reports to Rollbar
481	- **Sensitive data masking**: Automatically masks passwords, tokens, auth keys
482	- **Custom formatters**: Optional custom formatters for params/responses
483	
484	**Usage:**
485	
486	```typescript
487	import { LogExecution } from '@decorators/log-execution.decorator';
488	
489	@Injectable()
490	export class MyService {
491		// Basic usage - logs everything with defaults
492		@LogExecution()
493		async myMethod(param: string): Promise<Result> {
494			// method implementation
495		}
496	
497		// With custom formatters (for large payloads or 3rd party responses)
498		@LogExecution({
499			paramFormatter: (params: { styleId: string }) => ({ styleId: params.styleId }),
500			responseFormatter: () => '', // Don't log large response
501		})
502		async fetchFromThirdParty({ styleId }: { styleId: string }): Promise<LargeData> {
503			// 3rd party API call
504		}
505	
506		// With custom options
507		@LogExecution({
508			logParams: true,
509			logResponse: false,
510			logExecutionTime: true,
511			logErrToRollbar: true,
512			maskSensitiveData: true,
513		})
514		async sensitiveOperation(data: SensitiveData): Promise<void> {
515			// sensitive operation
516		}
517	}
518	```
519	
520	**When to use:**
521	
522	- ✅ All 3rd party API requests (Algolia, MMS, AWS, OpenAI, etc.)
523	- ✅ Important business logic methods
524	- ✅ Database operations that may be slow
525	- ✅ Methods that handle sensitive data
526	- ✅ Methods with complex error scenarios
527	- ❌ Simple getters/setters
528	- ❌ Private utility methods
529	- ❌ Methods called in tight loops
530	
531	**Configuration options:**
532	
533	- `logParams`: Log method parameters (default: `true`)
534	- `logResponse`: Log method response (default: `true`)
535	- `logExecutionTime`: Log execution time (default: `true`)
536	- `logErrToRollbar`: Log errors to Rollbar (default: `true`)
537	- `logLongExecution`: Log long execution to Rollbar (default: `true`)
538	- `rollbarLevel`: Rollbar log level (default: `'debug'`)
539	- `maskSensitiveData`: Mask sensitive keys (default: `true`)
540	- `sensitiveKeys`: Array of sensitive key names to mask
541	- `customContext`: Additional context to include in logs
542	- `paramFormatter`: Custom formatter function for parameters
543	- `responseFormatter`: Custom formatter function for response
544	
545	**Default sensitive keys masked:**
546	`password`, `pass`, `token`, `secret`, `auth`, `authorization`, `cookie`, `session`, `credentials`, `apiKey`, `_cookie`, `csrf`, `_csrf`
547	
548	## Configuration Files
549	
550	### TypeScript Configuration (tsconfig.json)
551	
552	- **Target**: ES2023 with strict type checking
553	- **Strict options**: All enabled including `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`
554	- **Path aliases**: Configured for clean imports (see Import Aliases section)
555	- **Incremental compilation**: Enabled for faster builds
556	- **strictPropertyInitialization**: Disabled (due to NestJS dependency injection)
557	
558	### ESLint Configuration (eslint.config.mjs)
559	
560	**When writing code, follow these ESLint rules:**
561	
562	- **No console**: Use Logger instead of `console.log`
563	- **No relative imports**: Use path aliases only (`@modules/`, `@utils/`, etc.)
564	- **Explicit return types**: All functions must have explicit return types (`@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types`)
565	- **Import order**: Enforced with newlines between groups (builtin → external → internal → type)
566	- **TypeScript strict**: All strict TypeScript rules enabled
567	- **Test files**: Relaxed rules for `*.spec.ts` files (allow `any`, relative imports)
568	- **Import newlines**: Required after imports (`import/newline-after-import`)
569	- **No duplicates**: Duplicate imports not allowed (`import/no-duplicates`)
570	
571	# Testing
572	
573	- **Unit**: `*.unit-spec.ts` in `/src/unit-tests/`
574	- **E2E**: `*.e2e-spec.ts` in `/test/`
575	- **Coverage**: Separate reports in `/coverage-unit/` and `/coverage-e2e/`
576	- **DB**: MySQL replica with auto-reset for e2e tests
577	- **Load Testing**: Artillery configuration in `/src/scripts/load-testing/`
578	
579	### Coverage Requirements
580	
581	All test suites must maintain **95% minimum coverage** across all metrics:
582	
583	- **Branches**: 95%
584	- **Functions**: 95%
585	- **Lines**: 95%
586	- **Statements**: 95%
587	
588	**Configuration:**
589	
590	- Coverage thresholds enforced in `test/jest.coverage.config.ts`
591	- Unit tests ignore `/modules/db/` patterns
592	- E2E tests collect coverage only from `*.(controller|service).ts` files
593	- Tests will fail if coverage falls below thresholds
594	- Run `npm run test:coverage` to verify all coverage metrics
595	
596	## Key Dependencies
597	
598	### Core
599	
600	- **@nestjs/\*** - NestJS framework and modules
601	- **@nestjs/platform-fastify** - Fastify adapter for performance
602	- **knex** + **mysql2** - Database query builder and MySQL driver
603	- **nest-knexjs** - Knex integration for NestJS
604	
605	### Caching & Queue
606	
607	- **ioredis** + **@keyv/redis** - Redis client and keyv adapter
608	- **redlock** - Distributed locking
609	- **@aws-sdk/client-sqs** + **sqs-consumer** - AWS SQS integration
610	
611	### Monitoring & Logging
612	
613	- **newrelic** - Application performance monitoring
614	- **rollbar** - Error tracking
615	- **correlation-id-ts** - Request correlation tracking
616	
617	### AI & Search
618	
619	- **openai** - OpenAI API integration
620	- **algoliasearch** - Algolia search integration
621	- **@google-cloud/bigquery** - BigQuery integration
622	
623	### Utilities
624	
625	- **class-transformer** + **class-validator** - DTO validation and transformation
626	- **color-diff** - Color comparison utilities
627	- **date-holidays** - Holiday date calculations
628	
629	## Deployment
630	
631	- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions — merge to `main` triggers automatic deploy
632	- **Migration timing**: `ktool` deployment order is new code pods start → health checks pass → migration runs → old pods drain. New code runs against the old schema until migration completes (see Database Migrations section above).
633	
634	## Workflow Skills
635	
636	Invoke these skills with `/skill-name` or rely on Claude to invoke them automatically:
637	
638	| Skill | When to use |
639	|-------|-------------|
640	| `/starting-jira-tasks` | Begin work on a Jira task — fetches ticket, sets up branch |
641	| `/new-worktree` | Create an isolated git worktree for a RECO task |
642	| `/fix-eslint-workflow` | Fix ESLint violations in a file (named params, aliases, return types) |
643	| `/test-skill` | Create or fix unit/e2e tests |
644	| `/code-quality-skill` | Run lint + format + type-check and auto-fix |
645	| `/database-skill` | Schema design, migrations, models, query optimization |
646	| `/nestjs-skill` | NestJS patterns, DI, performance, TypeScript configs |
647	| `/documentation-skill` | Create or update docs in `docs/` |
648	| `/create-pr` | Create a PR with Jira validation |
649	| `/verify` | Pre-PR check — runs full test suite and confirms all checks pass |
650	| `/diagram-skill` | Generate ASCII sequence/UML diagrams |
651

content.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me analyze the current state of `@LogExecution` usage and identify the gaps.

**Current state summary:**

**Trending Products module:**
- `trending-products-dashboard.controller.ts`: `getTrendingProductsSiteWide` has `@LogExecution` (line 70), `getTrendingProductsByCategory` has `@LogExecution` (line 103), `getDashboard` does NOT have it (but it's just serving a static HTML file - not a 3rd party call)
- `trending-products.service.ts`: `getTrendingProductsSiteWide` has `@LogExecution` (line 106), `saveBatchResults` has `@LogExecution` (line 359). **Missing**: `getTrendingProductsByCategory` (public, calls DB + hydration service), `getStyleIdsInDateRange` (public, calls DB)
- `render-trending-results.service.ts`: `generateDetailedTrendingProductsReports` has `@LogExecution` (line 52). No 3rd party calls - it's a file writer.

**Frequently Bought Together module:**
- `frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`: All 3 controller methods have `@LogExecution` already.
- `frequently-bought-together.service.ts`: `getFrequentlyBoughtTogetherV2` has `@LogExecution` (line 429). **Missing**: `getFrequentlyBoughtTogether` (public, makes multiple DB/service calls), `getFrequentlyBoughtTogetherByRootCategories` (public, makes multiple DB/service calls)

Now I have all the information needed. Let me note that this is a **read-only** task and I cannot write files. I will produce the plan as output text.

---

Here is the complete implementation plan for RECO-813.

---

## Implementation Plan: RECO-813 - Return logs back for 3rd party requests

### 1. Summary

RECO-813 restores `@LogExecution` decorator coverage on public methods in the `trending-products` and `frequently-bought-together` modules that make 3rd party or cross-service calls (database queries via injected services, product hydration, category correlation lookups). The decorator was either removed or never added to several methods. Adding it back ensures these calls are visible in production logs with execution time tracking, parameter logging, and Rollbar error reporting.

### 2. Scope

- **Single PR** targeting branch `RECO-813-return-logs-3rd-party-requests`, base `RECO-808-fix-eslint-trending-products-service`
- **No new dependencies, no env vars, no DB changes, no business logic changes**
- **Files to modify (3 files total):**
  1. `src/modules/rest/trending-products/trending-products.service.ts`
  2. `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts`
  3. _(No changes needed to controllers or render-trending-results.service.ts -- they already have full coverage)_

### 3. Ordered Task Breakdown

#### Task 1: Add `@LogExecution` to `TrendingProductsService.getTrendingProductsByCategory` (2 min)

**File:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/trending-products/trending-products.service.ts`
**Method:** `getTrendingProductsByCategory` (line 33)
**Why:** This public method calls `forecastedTrendingProductsService.getTrendingProductsByCategory()` (DB query) and `productHydrationService.hydrateProductsWithColors()` (cross-service call that hits the styles MV table). Its sibling method `getTrendingProductsSiteWide` already has the decorator.

**Decorator config:**
```
@LogExecution({
    paramFormatter: ([params]: [{ categoryId: number; excludeProductIds?: number[]; limit?: number; offset?: number }]) => ({
        categoryId: params.categoryId,
        excludeProductIds: params.excludeProductIds,
        limit: params.limit,
        offset: params.offset,
    }),
    responseFormatter: (res: TrendingProductsByCategoryResponseDto) => ({
        trendingProductsCount: res.trendingProducts.length,
        categoryId: res.categoryId,
    }),
})
```

**Rationale for options:** Mirror the pattern used on `getTrendingProductsSiteWide` at line 106. Log params to see which category and exclusions. Log response count (not full product objects) to keep log size manageable.

#### Task 2: Add `@LogExecution` to `TrendingProductsService.getStyleIdsInDateRange` (2 min)

**File:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/trending-products/trending-products.service.ts`
**Method:** `getStyleIdsInDateRange` (line 178)
**Why:** This public method calls `productAnnualSalesService.findByYearRange()` (DB query to product_annual_sales table). Used by CLI forecasting commands.

**Decorator config:**
```
@LogExecution({
    paramFormatter: ([params]: [{ startDate: string; endDate: string }]) => ({
        startDate: params.startDate,
        endDate: params.endDate,
    }),
    responseFormatter: (res: IProductAnnualSalesBase[]) => ({
        count: res.length,
    }),
})
```

**Rationale:** The response is an array of sales records which can be large. Only log the count.

#### Task 3: Add `@LogExecution` to `FrequentlyBoughtTogetherService.getFrequentlyBoughtTogether` (3 min)

**File:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts`
**Method:** `getFrequentlyBoughtTogether` (line 58)
**Why:** This public method makes multiple 3rd party / cross-service calls:
- `stylesService.getStyleById()` (DB) -- called in a loop for each styleId
- `categoryCorrelationsService.getTopCorrelatedCategories()` (DB) -- called per style
- `stylesService.getMostPopularCategories()` (DB fallback)
- `stylesService.getMostPopularStylesByCategories()` (DB)
- `productHydrationService.hydrateProducts()` (cross-service, DB)
- Self-call to `getFrequentlyBoughtTogetherByRootCategories()` (fallback path)

**Decorator config:**
```
@LogExecution({
    paramFormatter: ([params]: [FrequentlyBoughtTogetherQueryDto]) => ({
        styleIds: params.styleIds,
        excludeCount: params.exclude?.length ?? 0,
    }),
    responseFormatter: (res: FrequentlyBoughtTogetherResponseDto) => ({
        count: res.frequently_bought_together.length,
        fallbackUsed: res.fallback_used,
    }),
})
```

**Rationale:** Mirror the pattern from `getFrequentlyBoughtTogetherV2` at line 429 and the controller's formatter at line 33. Log styleIds (small array, typically 1-5 items) and response count.

#### Task 4: Add `@LogExecution` to `FrequentlyBoughtTogetherService.getFrequentlyBoughtTogetherByRootCategories` (3 min)

**File:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts`
**Method:** `getFrequentlyBoughtTogetherByRootCategories` (line 302)
**Why:** This public method makes multiple 3rd party / cross-service calls:
- `stylesService.getStyleById()` (DB) -- called in a loop
- `rootCategoriesCorrelationsService.getTopCorrelatedCategories()` (DB) -- called per style
- `stylesService.getMostPopularRootCategories()` (DB fallback)
- `stylesService.getMostPopularStylesByRootCategories()` (DB)
- `productHydrationService.hydrateProducts()` (cross-service, DB)

**Decorator config:**
```
@LogExecution({
    paramFormatter: ([params]: [FrequentlyBoughtTogetherRootCategoriesQueryDto]) => ({
        styleIds: params.styleIds,
        excludeCount: params.exclude?.length ?? 0,
    }),
    responseFormatter: (res: FrequentlyBoughtTogetherRootCategoriesResponseDto) => ({
        count: res.frequently_bought_together.length,
        fallbackUsed: res.fallback_used,
    }),
})
```

**Rationale:** Identical pattern to Task 3, matching the controller's formatter at line 64.

#### Task 5: Run code quality checks (2 min)

Run `npm run code-quality-check` to verify lint, format, and type-check all pass after the decorator additions.

### 4. Test Strategy

**Existing test coverage situation:**
- There are **no unit test files** for `TrendingProductsService` or `FrequentlyBoughtTogetherService` in `src/unit-tests/`.
- There are e2e test files at:
  - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/test/rest/trending-products.service.e2e-spec.ts`
  - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/test/rest/trending-products-dashboard.controller.e2e-spec.ts`
  - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/test/frequently-bought-together.e2e-spec.ts`
  - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-813/test/frequently-bought-together-root-categories.e2e-spec.ts`

**Test approach:**
- `@LogExecution` is a pure decorator that wraps methods transparently. It does not change return values or behavior.
- The decorator itself already has its own unit test at `src/unit-tests/log-execution.decorator.unit-spec.ts`.
- **No new unit tests are needed** for the decorator additions -- the decorator is already tested, and adding it does not change method signatures or behavior.
- **Run existing e2e tests** to confirm nothing breaks: `npm run test:e2e` (the e2e specs exercise the actual endpoints and will surface any decorator misconfiguration such as wrong paramFormatter signature).
- If coverage requirements demand unit tests for these service files (currently there are none), that would be a separate ticket scope -- not part of this logging-only change.

### 5. Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| `paramFormatter` type mismatch causes runtime error | Low | Medium | Follow exact pattern from existing `@LogExecution` usages in same files. The `[params]` destructuring pattern is consistent across the codebase. |
| `responseFormatter` accesses undefined property on error path | Low | Low | The decorator catches errors before calling `responseFormatter`. Response formatter only runs on success. |
| Logging large payloads slows down requests | Low | Low | All `responseFormatter` functions log only counts/booleans, never full product arrays. |
| Double-logging (decorator + existing manual `this.logger` calls) | Certain | None | The existing manual `this.logger.debug/log` calls inside these methods provide fine-grained step-by-step logging. `@LogExecution` adds entry/exit/timing logging at the method boundary. Both are useful and complementary. No need to remove the manual logs. |
| Breaking existing e2e tests | Very Low | Medium | Decorator is transparent. Run full e2e suite to confirm. |

### 6. Methods NOT needing `@LogExecution` (for completeness)

The following methods were reviewed and intentionally excluded:

- **`TrendingProductsService.forecastStyleTrend`** -- Pure computational method (no 3rd party calls). It does math on in-memory data.
- **`TrendingProductsService.invalidateLatestForecasts/deactivateOldPredictions/activateLatestPredictions`** -- These are thin delegation wrappers to `forecastedTrendingProductsService`. The underlying DB service methods should have their own logging. These are CLI-only methods.
- **`TrendingProductsDashboardController.getDashboard`** -- Serves a static HTML file from disk. Not a 3rd party call.
- **`RenderTrendingResultsService.generateDetailedTrendingProductsReports`** -- Already has `@LogExecution` (line 52).
- **`FrequentlyBoughtTogetherService.getCurrentColorStyle`** -- Private method. Per CLAUDE.md guidelines, private utility methods should not have `@LogExecution`.
- **All private methods** (`selectRandomStylesFromCategories`, `selectRandomStylesFromRootCategories`, etc.) -- Private, no direct 3rd party calls.
